markets.question: BNB Up or Down - May 2, 8:00AM-12:00PM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the BNB price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BNB/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/bnb-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BNB/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Swindon Town FC vs. Chesterfield FC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming League Two game between Swindon Town FC and Chesterfield FC, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Swindon Town FC and Chesterfield FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Swindon Town FC vs. Chesterfield FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the League Two game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Swindon Town holding sixth in the League Two table on 75 points after 44 games and Chesterfield lurking two points back in seventh, this final-day County Ground clash on May 2 carries massive playoff implications for both promotion hopefuls. Swindon, boasting a strong home record and a 2-1 victory in November's reverse fixture, face challenges from recent injuries to centre-back Ryan Tafazolli (fractured elbow) and midfielder Gavin Kilkenny (training issue), both potentially sidelined. Chesterfield, unbeaten in six with five wins in their last seven away outings, miss only Janoi Donacien but bring momentum from gritty draws and triumphs over Grimsby and Cheltenham, positioning traders to weigh home advantage against visitor form in the promotion race.

markets.question: Spread: Moreirense FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Moreirense FC" if Moreirense FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "CF Estrela da Amadora".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Moreirense FC vs. CF Estrela da Amadora - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primeira Liga game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Moreirense hold a solid seventh place in the Primeira Liga standings with 39 points from 30 matches, bolstered by a recent 1-0 home win over Estoril that ended a three-game skid, showcasing defensive resilience at Parque de Jogos Comendador Joaquim de Almeida Freitas. Estrela da Amadora languish in 15th, fighting relegation with a poor away record, following their goalless draw against Moreirense in December. Head-to-head favors Moreirense overall (5 wins in 10), though recent meetings are tight. Minor injuries sideline Moreirense's Vasco Sousa (long-term fibula) and Estrela's Jefferson Encada (muscle since March), but full squads expected; late-season stakes heighten Estrela's motivation amid table pressures and home/away splits.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and JD Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Team WE vs JD Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 2 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Team WE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: JD Gaming holds a commanding edge over Team WE in their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch, fresh off a 2-1 series win against the same opponent just one week ago on April 25, which extended JDG's five-series win streak amid strong mid-table positioning at 5-3 overall (11-7 games). Team WE, mired in a six-series loss streak including a recent defeat to Bilibili Gaming, struggles with consistency despite snagging a game in their last meeting, ranking near the bottom of the eight-team Group Ascend where top-four finishers secure direct playoff berths. Historical head-to-head favors JDG at 88% win rate, with no reported roster changes or patch shifts altering the stylistic matchup dynamics.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Invictus Gaming and Anyone's Legend each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend enters this LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch as trader consensus favorite over Invictus Gaming, bolstered by their 2-0 sweep of IG on April 28 and superior 6-3 series record (12-7 games) compared to IG's 4-5 (10-12 games). Both squads stumbled yesterday—AL fell 0-2 to Top Esports, IG dropped 1-2 to Weibo Gaming—potentially disrupting AL's four-win streak while highlighting IG's inconsistent mid-table form amid off-season additions like Breathe and Fury. Top-four positioning offers direct playoffs qualification in the double round-robin Group Ascend, with AL's stylistic edges in skirmishes and veteran top laner Flandre's MVP performances fueling optimism despite the quick turnaround.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Invictus Gaming and Anyone's Legend each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Dobropillia by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Dobropillia by June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

This market refers to the city of Dobropillia (48.47, 37.08), which borders Sviatohorivka and Hannivka. Other Ukrainian towns/cities named Dobropillia will not be considered for resolution.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of Dobropillia is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

Derby took an early lead through Sammie Szmodics in the 5th minute amid their desperate play-off push from 8th place (69 points), but a backpass error allowed Tom Cannon to equalize in the 62nd, followed by Sydie Peck's winner seven minutes later, boosting mid-table Sheffield United (15th, 57 points) who had won the prior two head-to-heads. With the match concluded and no appeals or disputes reported, no realistic scenarios like red cards, VAR overturns, or postponements remain to challenge the outcome, as resolution awaits league confirmation.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Houston be between 54-55°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the William P. Hobby Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the William P. Hobby Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts and ensemble model consensus from NOAA project Houston's highest temperature on May 4 exceeding 80°F, aligning with the 99.6% trader consensus for 68°F or higher amid building high pressure and clearing skies following recent cooler, overcast conditions around 57°F. This positioning reflects early May climatological normals of 83.4°F at Houston's Intercontinental Airport, where historical lowest highs bottom at 70°F in rare 1954 cold snaps, far above lower-binned outcomes. Long-range outlooks like the Old Farmer's Almanac anticipate warmer-than-average conditions (+3°F). Realistic challenges include an unforeseen northerly cold front or persistent marine stratus suppressing peaks below 68°F, though current guidance shows low likelihood; watch twice-daily NWS Houston/Galveston updates for refinements.

Charlize Theron's star power in this high-stakes mountaineering action thriller, paired with Netflix's track record for blockbuster action hits, has fueled massive streaming metrics far outpacing holdovers like Roommates and Bugonia. markets.question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 2 at 9:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "1. FC Heidenheim 1846".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Bayern München vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 2 at 9:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich hold a commanding 12-point lead atop the Bundesliga table after 29 matches, with Heidenheim mired in the relegation zone on 19 points from four wins, facing a must-win survival battle away at the Allianz Arena. Bayern's dominance is underscored by recent 4-0 head-to-head routs and superior home form, though trader consensus reflects caution amid a mounting injury list—Serge Gnabry sidelined long-term by an adductor tear, joined by Lennart Karl (hamstring) and others—thinning attacking depth during a congested schedule featuring Champions League semis against PSG. Heidenheim contend without key forwards Sirlord Conteh (knee) and Mikkel Kaufmann (calf), amplifying Bayern's stylistic edge despite rotation risks.

events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 55.5% implied probability as defending champions with a league-best 64 regular-season wins, having swept Phoenix Suns 4-0 in the first round behind Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's scoring and Chet Holmgren's rim protection, positioning them strongly for Western Conference semifinals. San Antonio Spurs sit at 19.9% after clinching their first playoff series victory since 2017 by ousting Portland Trail Blazers, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's dominant two-way play and offensive versatility. Boston Celtics hold 9.3% amid a tight first-round battle, dropping Game 6 to Philadelphia 76ers and facing a decisive Game 7 with home-court advantage. Eastern series like Detroit Pistons-Orlando Magic tied 3-3 limit other contenders' paths against surging West powers.

markets.description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Aryna Sabalenka as the slim 2026 Women's Wimbledon favorite at 29% implied probability, buoyed by her dominant 25-1 record this season, including deep Madrid Open runs and Indian Wells triumph over Elena Rybakina, showcasing the big serving and power that thrives on grass. Rybakina trails closely at 23.5% after her Stuttgart title and career-high No. 2 ranking, leveraging her 2022 Wimbledon win and 27 match victories YTD for grass pedigree. Iga Świątek's 16% reflects a title-less 13-7 start with hard-court inconsistencies, historically capping her grass breakthroughs at quarterfinals. The bunched top reflects grass-court unpredictability, absent tune-ups pre-French Open, mutual stylistic edges in flat-hitting serves, and lingering injury concerns like Sabalenka's recent Stuttgart withdrawal, opening paths for grass specialists like Amanda Anisimova amid a deep WTA field.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 26% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by his commanding edge in early primary polls like a March POLITICO survey trouncing Kamala Harris in their home state, bolstered by his executive record and national anti-Trump profile post-2024. Former Vice President Harris follows at 10%, gaining from her April signals of interest at the National Action Network convention and a recent poll surge after testing messaging in battleground states. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's 8.5% reflects progressive enthusiasm, while Jon Ossoff's 6.6% highlights Georgia's swing-state appeal. In this wide-open field, governors like Newsom and Josh Shapiro differentiate via proven turnout in red-leaning states; consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results, fundraising hauls, and DNC endorsements ahead of early primaries.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump visit China on May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the calendar date (ET) on which U.S. President Donald Trump next visits China.

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Trump visit China on...?

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: White House-confirmed preparations, including U.S. Air Force C-17 Globemaster cargo flights landing at Beijing Capital International Airport on May 1-2 with transponders off, signal firm logistics for President Trump's summit with Xi Jinping, positioning May 13 as trader consensus for arrival at 71.5% implied probability. The trip, rescheduled from late March amid the Iran war per March 25 announcements from Reuters, AP, and Politico, targets May 14-15 bilateral talks on trade tensions, Strait of Hormuz restrictions, and regional stability. Recent Chinese warnings on Taiwan and Trump's insistence the visit proceeds as planned have compressed no-visit-by-May-31 odds to 4.6%, reflecting diplomatic momentum despite lingering uncertainties like Hormuz de-escalation.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Taylor Pendrith win the 2026 Cadillac Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Cadillac Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Cadillac Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Cadillac Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 9, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

Jordan Spieth, Alex Smalley, and Nick Taylor share T2 at 8-under—five strokes adrift—fueling their 5.5%, 3.7%, and 2.7% shares, while Gary Woodland's solo fifth at 7-under supports 2.3%. Round 3 tees off amid calm conditions, with chasers needing birdie runs to close the gap.

markets.question: Will ForecastEx self-certify sports event contracts by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: CFTC's March 2026 advisory (Letter 26-08) has sharpened focus on Designated Contract Markets self-certifying sports event contracts, mandating pre-listing staff consultations, detailed manipulation risk analyses, and reliable settlement data to ensure market integrity. Established DCMs like CME and Kalshi have already listed hundreds of such prediction market contracts, capitalizing on hedging demand from sports leagues and broadcasters, while challengers including Railbird and Aristotle weigh commercial viability against regulatory hurdles. State attorneys general and tribal gaming interests decry these as encroaching on sportsbooks, fueling litigation risks. With the June 30 deadline looming, traders eye upcoming CFTC responses to recent ANPRM comments for catalysts that could prompt or deter further certifications.

markets.question: Will Meta have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (LOW) $3.75 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the next model released by OpenAI debut at a score of at least 1500?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next model released by OpenAI has at least the specified score on the Arena.AI Leaderboard (arena.ai/leaderboard/text) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on the date after the day of the release, 12:00 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If no qualifying score for the specified model is available on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If no qualifying score becomes available by the end of the seventh day following the day of the model’s release, or if no qualifying model release occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

If multiple models are released on the same calendar date or if multiple variants of the specified model appear on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at the relevant check time (e.g., base, “Thinking,” or “Instant”), the highest-scoring variant will be used for resolution.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at arena.ai/leaderboard/text. If this resolution source is unavailable at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If it remains unavailable through the end of the seventh day after a qualifying release, it will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

No public S-1 disclosure or further updates in the ensuing four months have eroded confidence in a near-term float, with just two months remaining to resolution. markets.question: Will Xi Jinping purge Wang Huning in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if either of the following conditions are met by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

1) The listed individual is removed or resigns from their primary political post or from their position, if any, on the Chinese Politburo or its standing committee, with a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor.

2) The listed individual is expelled from the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). 

Qualifying announcements and reporting of a purge before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal/expulsion goes into effect.

A listed individual leaving office at the end of a regularly scheduled term, or being removed/resigning from office without a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor, will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from the Chinese Government and a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will USD reach 2.0M Iranian rials by May 31?

This market will resolve according to the daily finalized free-market USD exchange rate as displayed on Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd), which publishes prices in Iranian toman, where 1 Iranian toman equals 10 Iranian rials (IRR).

Bayern, unbeaten domestically this season atop the Frauen-Bundesliga save for a 7-1 group-stage drubbing by Barcelona in October 2025, advanced via a gritty 5-3 aggregate over Manchester City with late strikes from Viggósdóttir and Dallmann. First leg on April 25 in Munich favors Bayern's home crowd and revenge motive, but Barcelona's depth shines despite Aitana Bonmatí's ongoing fibula absence until early May; Bayern miss winger Klara Bühl (muscle injury). Head-to-head favors Barça (4-1-0), underscoring trader focus on aggregate dynamics, clean sheets, and Salma Paralluelo's form.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 40-59 Truth Social posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

events.title: Donald Trump # Truth Social posts April 28 - May 5, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a neck-and-neck race between 80-99 posts (41%) and 100-119 posts (40%) for President Donald Trump's Truth Social activity from April 28 to May 5, driven by a current tally of 52-58 posts through early May 2, averaging 11-13 daily amid variability. A spike of 38 posts on May 1—tied to commentary on Iran tensions and domestic issues—lifted the pace from quieter starts like zero on April 28, while archives confirm six on April 29 and 14 on April 30. This pace projects 90-110 total, but historical averages of 18-22 daily leave room for surges. Remaining days (May 3-5) could tip the balance via foreign policy escalations, White House announcements, or characteristic late-night sprees, against quieter periods.

markets.question: Will Giannis Antetokounmpo play for the Atlanta Hawks in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team Giannis Antetokounmpo officially joins by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo does not officially join a new team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Milwaukee Bucks”.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo retires or is not under contract with any professional team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official acquisition announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the Milwaukee Bucks and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in San Francisco be between 60-61°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the San Francisco International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the San Francisco International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/san-francisco/KSFO.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service guidance indicates a forecasted high near 64°F for San Francisco on May 4 amid persistent "May Gray" conditions, with morning marine stratus delaying burn-off and capping afternoon warming under steady onshore flow. Recent days reflect this pattern, as May 1 logged a morning low of 54°F with partial clearing insufficient for peaks above the low 60s, aligning with climatological norms where early May highs average 65°F at KSFO station. NOAA model ensembles show limited ridging aloft, reducing intensification potential, though deeper marine layers could trim 2-3°F. Traders eye evening forecast updates and hourly observations for shifts, given historical variability from full fog dissipation.

markets.question: Will Jonas Wind be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Goals scored in Bundesliga matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., DFB-Pokal, German Super Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Bundesliga season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The Bayern Munich striker, who netted in recent outings against Mainz and Stuttgart, benefits from his side's title-clinched dominance (82 points after 31 games) and record 113 league goals, minimizing rest risks despite upcoming Champions League semis and DFB-Pokal final. Challengers like Serhou Guirassy and Luis Díaz (both 15) face mathematical impossibility without Kane's prolonged absence via injury, an unlikely scenario given his fitness and Bayern's motivation to pad stats.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy faces Wave Esports in a pivotal BO3 Swiss Round 1 matchup at Exort Series #25 Main Stage, with both squads sporting recent roster overhauls—Spirit's young academy lineup (average age 17.6) rebuilt in February around veteran rifler Kiryasoo, while Wave (#149 HLTV) integrated Belof, Katalic, and sheezy in March following mixed March results like early Urban Riga Open exits. Wave advanced 3-1 from Contenders Stage, but Spirit Academy's Team Spirit backing and cleaner form trajectory fuel trader favoritism. A prior Wave 2-0 win over Spirit Academy Green in Exort #17 adds upset potential, as map vetoes on Dust2, Mirage, and Ancient could exploit stylistic edges in this online C-Tier bracket opener.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast updates from monitoring agencies like AccuWeather and the China Meteorological Administration show Shanghai's May 3 minimum temperature clustering around 14-15°C, driving trader consensus with 15°C at 32% implied probability and 14°C at 21%, amid considerable cloudiness and westerly breezes up to 21 km/h that moderate radiational cooling overnight. This marks a dip from May 2's expected 17°C low under partly sunny, southeasterly winds, reflecting a passing upper-level trough introducing cooler mid-level air. High uncertainty stems from variable cloud cover—persistent overcast could lift lows toward 17°C (20.5%), while unexpected clearing and light winds might drop to 13°C or below (3.7% combined)—with model ensembles like ECMWF and GFS showing spread. Watch CMA evening bulletins and station observations at Pudong for resolution-defining data.

markets.question: Will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit (HIGH) $95 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the VA-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the VA-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

No major polls have emerged post-redistricting, but the structural change has sidelined Republican chances ahead of the August 4 primaries and November general election, with GOP challengers facing an uphill battle in the new electoral math.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Encore Medical’s market cap be between $50M and $55M at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Encore Medical’s market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on April 22 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before June 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency.

It is calculated as the total number of outstanding shares, multiplied by the official closing share price of the publicly traded class on the first trading day.

If necessary, to accurately capture the company’s total market capitalization, rather than a stock-class-specific market capitalization, the calculation will include all outstanding share classes and apply any stated conversion ratios to the publicly traded class. Where no conversion right exists, such shares will be counted at their stated outstanding amount without discount, unless official filings explicitly specify differently.

The number of outstanding shares will be determined from official company filings or disclosures (e.g., SEC filings). The closing share price on the first trading day will be determined from the primary exchange’s official listing page.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official company filings and the primary exchange’s official listing page. The market capitalization will be determined through appropriate calculation using the total outstanding shares and the closing price from the first day of trading.

In the event of an interruption in the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that day as the first day of trading for the purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket reflects growing skepticism toward Encore Medical's IPO timeline and valuation, with no IPO before June 2026 leading at 37% implied probability amid repeated delays. The medtech firm, developing transcatheter PFO closure devices, filed its S-1 in September 2025 with multiple amendments, missing the targeted April 22, 2026 NYSE American listing despite late-April rumors of a May 6 debut at $5 per share for 3 million shares to raise $15 million. TTM revenue rose 49% to $2.1 million with 40% gross margins, but a $1.6 million net loss and ongoing FDA pivotal trial (target completion 2027) cap enthusiasm for higher brackets like 55M+ at just 1.8%. Upcoming pricing and SEC effectiveness remain key catalysts.

markets.question: Will Jared Kushner attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire holding since April 8 after the US-initiated conflict with Iran over its nuclear program and Strait of Hormuz disruptions, Tehran submitted a new peace proposal via mediator Pakistan on May 1, offering to ease shipping blockades in parallel with US de-escalation and postponing nuclear negotiations. President Trump rejected it as insufficient, signaling potential military escalation while diplomats pursue resumed direct talks, possibly next week in Islamabad. Prior April Islamabad round featured US Vice President JD Vance, envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff facing Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf; trader consensus weighs repeat high-level US attendance against Trump's dissatisfaction and demands for strict nuclear curbs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ecuador's strong CONMEBOL qualifying campaign, featuring key contributions from Premier League stars like Moisés Caicedo and veteran forward Enner Valencia, positions them as clear trader favorites at 77% implied probability against World Cup debutants Curaçao in this Group E clash at neutral Arrowhead Stadium. Curaçao's historic qualification via dramatic CONCACAF playoffs earned them a spot but highlights their underdog status, with limited high-level experience reflected in the 11% win pricing and 20.5% draw consensus. Recent training camps show clean injury reports for both sides, Ecuador sharpening rapid counters and set-piece execution while Curaçao emphasizes compact defending, though the step-up in class favors La Tri's tactical edge and depth.

markets.question: Sinner vs. Zverev: Match O/U 21.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Jannik Sinner and Alexander Zverev in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for May 3, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.

events.title: Madrid Open: Jannik Sinner vs Alexander Zverev
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Jannik Sinner and Alexander Zverev in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for May 3, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Jannik Sinner' if Jannik Sinner advances against Alexander Zverev.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Zverev' if Alexander Zverev advances against Jannik Sinner.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Jannik Sinner faces Alexander Zverev in the Mutua Madrid Open final on clay, chasing a record fifth consecutive ATP Masters 1000 title against Zverev's bid for a third crown at the event. Both secured spots Friday—Sinner edging Arthur Fils via a pivotal 24-shot rally break in the semifinal, Zverev cruising past surprise semifinalist Alexander Blockx. Sinner leads head-to-head 9-4, unbeaten in eight matches since 2024 including a dominant Monte Carlo semifinal last month. An early injury scare for Sinner dissipated with flawless play; Zverev's baseline power and Madrid history offer upset potential amid high-altitude conditions, but Sinner's return dominance shapes trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: The April 30, 2026, deadline for the "Nothing Ever Happens: April" market has passed without any triggering events materializing, cementing trader consensus at 100% implied probability for "Nothing." Despite early-month US-Iran tensions—including Iranian forces downing US F-15E and A-10 aircraft on April 3-4, President Trump's threats of strikes, and WTI crude surging past $112—de-escalation ensued with no US ground entry into Iran. The Federal Reserve held rates steady at its April 28-29 FOMC meeting, avoiding policy changes; rhetoric toward Cuba intensified without military action; and no qualifying Epstein-related jailings occurred in April, following prior February arrests. With the resolution window closed, only oracle disputes over event verification could theoretically alter outcomes, though none appear viable amid clear non-triggers.

markets.question: Will Meta have the second best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: NEC Nijmegen, entrenched in third place in the Eredivisie standings with 53 points from 29 matches, welcomes relegation-threatened SC Telstar (17th, 27 points from 30 games) to Goffertstadion on May 2. NEC's recent 3-2 upset victory over league leaders PSV underscores their strong momentum and top-four push, bolstered by solid home form and a dominant head-to-head record (7 wins to Telstar's 2, including a 2-2 draw earlier this season at Telstar). Telstar, however, languishes on a six-match winless streak, suffering heavy defeats like 0-3 to Heerenveen and 1-4 at Utrecht, exposing defensive frailties away. Injuries sideline NEC's Freek Entius and Telstar's Adil Lechkar, while late-season stakes heighten intensity for both survival and European qualification.

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Partido Liberal (PL) at 74.5% to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, which renews 54 positions (two per state), reflecting state-level polls showing PL candidates leading in key battlegrounds like Santa Catarina, São Paulo, and Paraná amid the Bolsonaro family's strong brand. PL entered 2026 as the Senate's largest bloc with 15 senators after recent defections, bolstering its momentum. Recent Senate defeats for President Lula's administration, including the April 29 rejection of Attorney-General Jorge Messias's Supreme Court nomination, underscore opposition strength, while tight presidential polls tying Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) to Lula further lift PL prospects over PT (2.7%) and centrão rivals like UNIÃO (12.3%) and REPUBLICANOS (7.2%). Late scandals or coalition shifts could narrow the gap ahead of the vote.

markets.question: FC Arouca vs. CD Santa Clara: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game between FC Arouca and CD Santa Clara, scheduled for May 2 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if FC Arouca and CD Santa Clara combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will China GDP growth in Q2 2026 be between 4.0% and 4.3%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to China's Y/Y Growth Rate (%) of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP" release for Q2 of 2026, scheduled for July 16, 2026.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

For the full release schedule, see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/202512/t20251226_1962154.html

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Buenos Aires be 16°C on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ar/ezeiza/SAEZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed or nominated by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

Nominations will qualify even if the individual is not confirmed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, term-limited out of office in January 2027, faces slim odds of joining the Trump administration by June 30 amid unconfirmed rumors rather than official actions. Recent Axios reporting on April 21 cited sources claiming DeSantis is seeking high-profile roles like Attorney General, Secretary of Defense, or even a Supreme Court seat, prompting Trump to tell reporters on May 1 that he "likes him a lot" and would consider a cabinet spot. However, no nomination, resignation announcement, or formal discussions have materialized in the past week, with DeSantis remaining focused on state duties. Traders' strong 86% No consensus reflects the tight two-month window, historical patterns of early-term cabinet fillings, and absence of momentum for a mid-governorship exit.

Historical midterm penalties for the president's party, tossup races in battlegrounds like Maine and North Carolina, and uncertain primaries could tip control; stronger GOP recruitments or economic tailwinds might solidify Thune's position.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

events.title: Will US crude oil reserves fall to __ by June 5?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) stocks fell by 7.1 million barrels to 397.9 million barrels in the week ending April 24, per the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, reflecting ongoing Department of Energy sales amid elevated Brent crude prices near $103 per barrel driven by Strait of Hormuz disruptions and insufficient OPEC+ output hikes. This marks continued drawdowns from prior weeks, with commercial crude inventories also plunging 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million on record exports and high refinery utilization at 16.1 million barrels per day. Traders eye further SPR releases ahead of summer driving season demand, with the next EIA report due May 6 potentially signaling acceleration toward lower thresholds by June 5 resolution, though policy shifts or de-escalation could stabilize levels.
